method_atom: an analytic perturbative computation of the leading interference (cross) term between two distinct one-loop amplitudes that contribute to the same final state, regularised via dimensional regularisation and reduced to a basis of master integrals with massive internal propagators, used to quantify how contributions from separate channels partially cancel and assess whether the net effect is numerically significant.
object_atom: Higgs boson production in association with two jets at the LHC, specifically the interference between the gluon-fusion and weak-boson-fusion production channels.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the analytic loop-level interference calculation via dimensional regularisation and massive master integrals; the domain-specific object is the gluon-fusion/weak-boson-fusion Higgs-plus-two-jet process at the LHC.
